linting.notes: Linting is comprehensive. CI also checks that go.mod/go.sum are tidy with 'go mod tidy' + git diff check.

testing.mock_patterns: Uses httptest.Server for mocking HTTP responses in unit tests
testing.notes: Unit tests run without infrastructure and all pass. Integration tests require a running MLflow server (auto-managed in CI via make targets). Integration tests use build tag '//go:build integration'.

gating_checks.name: lint
commands: go mod tidy + git diff check

ci.notes: All 5 jobs triggered on pull_request and push to main. All appear to be gating. Integration tests are fully automated in CI (they start MLflow server, run tests, clean up).

build.build_command: No explicit build command (Go builds on-demand for tests)
build.notes: Project uses Go 1.24. Makefile provides convenience targets. The project installs golangci-lint and other tools into bin/ directory via lazy installation targets.

conventions.test_naming: Test functions: Test* (e.g., TestAPIError_Error). Go standard convention.
conventions.import_style: Standard Go import grouping. Generated code excluded from linting.
conventions.build_tags: Integration tests use '//go:build integration' build tag
conventions.notes: Clean separation between unit tests (no build tag) and integration tests (integration build tag). Generated protobuf code lives in internal/gen/ and is excluded from linting.

setup_commands: apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq make git curl

lint_commands.notes: Installs golangci-lint v2.1.6 to bin/ then runs golangci-lint run ./... - 0 issues found
Runs go vet ./... - passed with no output
No formatting issues - returned empty (correct formatting)

test_commands.notes: All unit tests passed. 5 packages tested, 0 failures. Tests use race detector.

container_recipe.notes: Container validation was fully successful. All basic commands work. Integration tests not validated in container because they require MLflow server infrastructure (Python-based).

results.command: apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq make git curl
go mod download && go mod tidy
results.output_snippet:
Go modules downloaded
go: downloading github.com/google/go-cmp v0.7.0
Go mod tidy complete
results.notes: Go dependencies downloaded and tidied successfully
go vet passed with no issues
All unit tests passed across 5 packages

validation.summary: Container validation: FULL SUCCESS. System deps installed, Go modules downloaded, lint passed (0 issues), vet passed, format check passed, all unit tests passed (5 packages).

gaps: Integration tests require MLflow server infrastructure (Python-based, installed via uv). These are fully automated in CI but cannot be validated in a simple Go container without the MLflow Python server.
No explicit build command, but this is standard for Go libraries (compiled on-demand).
Some integration test variants require PostgreSQL or Docker (test/integration-ci-postgres uses docker to start postgres:16).
notes: This is a well-structured Go project with excellent test infrastructure. All basic validation (lint, vet, unit tests) works perfectly in a standard Go container. Integration tests are sophisticated (they auto-start MLflow servers with different backends) but are fully automated in CI. An agent can easily validate patches by running lint + unit tests in a golang:1.24 container.
